summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Flash Fame system, Avatar Upload, and Abrir Sala features. All backend APIs (42/42 tests passed - 100%) and frontend UI flows working correctly. Flash Fame displays 4 categories (individual, clan_semanal, clan_mensual, pareja). Avatar upload with camera button and 'Cambiar Foto' button functional. 'Abrir Sala' button replaced mic icon in bottom nav and is prominent in Mio tab.

minor.issue: Agora SDK shows 'invalid vendor key' error - audio connection fails but this is expected in test environment with test credentials (AGORA_APP_ID starts with 'E' which may be incorrect)

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/flash-fame/all returns all 4 categories (individual, clan_semanal, clan_mensual, pareja)
Backend: Flash Fame individual is Melvin_Live (top coins user)
Backend: Flash Fame clan_semanal shows Los Reyes
Backend: Flash Fame clan_mensual shows Los Reyes
Backend: Flash Fame pareja shows Melvin_Live & testuser_024929
Backend: POST /api/users/{user_id}/avatar returns 404 for invalid user
Backend: POST /api/users/{user_id}/avatar rejects non-image files (400)
Backend: POST /api/users/{user_id}/avatar successfully uploads image
Backend: Avatar persists after upload (GET user returns new avatar)
Backend: GET /api/rankings/coins returns users with level, clan_name, cp_partner
Backend: POST /api/rooms creates room successfully
Frontend: Login with Melvin_Live / test123 successful
Frontend: Dashboard Popular tab shows Flash Fame banner with crown icon
Frontend: Flash Fame banner shows 'Melvin_Live - 2,020,000,000 coins'
Frontend: Dashboard shows 3 Flash Fame cards (Clan Semanal, Pareja #1, Clan Mensual)
Frontend: Clan Semanal card shows 'Los Reyes' and is clickable
Frontend: Pareja #1 card shows 'Melvin_Live & testuser_024929' and is clickable
Frontend: Clan Mensual card shows 'Los Reyes' and is clickable
Frontend: Clicking Clan Semanal navigates to ClanesView
Frontend: Clicking Pareja #1 navigates to ParejasView
Frontend: User rankings show level (Lv.99), clan name, and CP partner info
Frontend: Dashboard Mio tab has prominent '+ Abrir Sala' button at top
Frontend: Bottom navigation has 'Abrir Sala' button (replaced mic icon)
Frontend: Clicking 'Abrir Sala' triggers room creation prompt 'Nombre de la sala:'
Frontend: Profile page shows avatar with camera button (📷) for upload
Frontend: Profile has 'Cambiar Foto' button
Frontend: Profile has hidden file input for avatar upload
Frontend: Profile shows clan info card (TEST_Clan_0ac8f5eb)
Frontend: Profile shows pareja info card (testuser_024929)
Frontend: Profile shows all badges dynamically (Dueño, Fundador, Admin, VIP, Verificado, Aristocrat IX, Top 1 Semanal)
Frontend: Profile shows stats (Level 99, Monedas, Diamantes, Total Gastado)
Frontend: Notification bell in header with badge count (4)
critical_code_review_comments: Note: Agora App ID in .env starts with 'E' (Eccc145929e240a2b26f696a3a2ce542) - verify if this is correct as it may cause 'invalid vendor key' errors
seed_data_creation: Flash Fame data populated from existing users/clans/parejas. Individual=Melvin_Live (2B coins), clan_semanal=Los Reyes, clan_mensual=Los Reyes, pareja=Melvin_Live & testuser_024929
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new features working: 1) Flash Fame system with 4 categories fully functional - banner shows Top 1 individual, 3 cards show clan_semanal, pareja, clan_mensual. Cards are clickable and navigate to respective views. 2) Avatar upload working - camera button on avatar, 'Cambiar Foto' button, hidden file input, POST /api/users/{user_id}/avatar endpoint. 3) 'Abrir Sala' button replaced mic icon in bottom nav and is prominent in Mio tab. Both trigger room creation prompt. 4) Profile shows clan/pareja info cards and dynamic badges. 5) User rankings show level, clan_name, cp_partner. Agora audio still shows 'invalid vendor key' error in test environment.
notes: The Flash Fame data is dynamically populated from the database: individual from top coins user, clan_semanal/clan_mensual from top clans by weekly/monthly coins, pareja from top CP by total_coins. Avatar upload saves files to /app/backend/uploads/ and returns URL like /api/uploads/avatar_xxx.png.
